data.content: Now I'll update `CouponService.cs` to inject `TimeProvider` and replace all `DateTime.UtcNow` usages:
data.reasoningText:
Now I need to migrate `CouponService.cs` to use `TimeProvider` instead of `DateTime.UtcNow`. I'll:

1. Inject `TimeProvider` into the constructor
2. Replace all `DateTime.UtcNow` calls with `_timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime`

Let me make the changes.

toolCalls.name: edit
edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-6bb90b2140014c80b0cc93f63e755342/fixtures/ready-to-migrate/Services/CouponService.cs", "old_str": "            CreatedAt = DateTime.UtcNow,\n            ExpiresAt = DateTime.UtcNow.AddDays(validDays)", "new_str": "            CreatedAt = _timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime,\n            ExpiresAt = _timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime.AddDays(validDays)"}

toolCalls.name: edit

data.content:
Done. All `DateTime.UtcNow` calls in `CouponService.cs` now use the injected `TimeProvider`. Changes:

1. Added `_timeProvider` field
2. Constructor accepts `TimeProvider` (already in your DI container)
3. All 4 `DateTime.UtcNow` usages → `_timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime`

`AuditLogger.cs` left untouched. In tests you can now use `Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider` to control time.
